title: SSOT Pipeline Config
description: Schema for config/pipeline.json. Every field listed here is consumed by either the Cloudflare Worker (worker/src/index.ts) or one of the reusable workflows in .github/workflows/. Bump `version` whenever the contract between Worker and workflows changes.

$schema.description: Editor IntelliSense hint. Points at the sibling pipeline.schema.json file.

version.description: Config schema version. Bump (and update the const here) when the contract between Worker and workflows changes — see CLAUDE.md note about templates/ssot.yml.

plan_marker.description: Prefix that identifies a plan comment in Linear. Worker checks `comment.body.startsWith(plan_marker)` to decide whether reactions/replies count as plan interactions.

needs_input_marker.description: Prefix that identifies a 'legitimately parked, awaiting user input' comment in Linear. linear-implement's skill posts a question comment starting with this when it can't proceed without the user (ambiguous requirement, false plan premise, nothing to do). The Worker /verify (early Stop-hook) treats its presence as a pass so the agent isn't nagged to open a PR; the workflow's backstop verify classifies the run as `awaiting_input` (no Stuck, no replan). See W-311.

agent_sessions_enabled.description: Feature flag (W-243) for Linear Agent Sessions. When true, the Worker routes AgentSessionEvent webhooks into the loop (acks with an activity + fires linear-pickup). Dormant by default — also requires the Linear app to have agent scopes + the 'Agent session events' webhook category enabled. See docs/agent-sessions.md.

linear_mcp_url.description: URL of the Linear MCP server passed into Claude Code prompts in workflows.

linear_mcp_transport.description: MCP transport mode for the Linear MCP server.

planning_state.description: Linear state name used during plan generation (linear-pickup, linear-replan).

plan_review_state.description: Linear state name after a plan has been posted and is awaiting approval.

in_progress_state.description: Linear state name when implementation is in flight. A human moving an issue here also triggers linear-implement (gated on approved_user_ids).

in_review_state.description: Linear state name when a PR has been opened and is awaiting review.

stuck_state.description: Linear state name used by workflow verification steps when something goes wrong.

approval_phrases.description: Phrases (case-insensitive) in a reply that count as plan approval and fast-path straight to linear-implement. Worker's matchesApprovalPhrase() uses word-boundary matching for word phrases (`\bphrase\b`, so `ship it` matches `Ship it!` but not `shipping it`) and substring matching for emoji-only phrases (👍/✅/🚀). Keep only UNAMBIGUOUS approvals here — short/common words like bare `go` are intentionally omitted so a change-request like `go ahead and tweak X` doesn't false-fire an implement; those ride linear-replan's LLM intent judge instead (W-132).

branch_prefix.description: Git branch prefix injected into prompts (e.g. `wells/`). Must end with `/`.

pr_fix_max_attempts.description: Max number of pr-fix retry attempts before giving up and flipping the issue to Stuck.

review_bot_login.description: GitHub login of the reviewer bot (e.g. `wr-claude-reviewer[bot]`). pr-review and pr-fix workflows compare against this.

fix_reviewer_logins.description: GitHub logins whose PR review comments trigger pr-fix.

approved_user_ids.description: Linear user IDs (UUIDs) whose approval counts when enforce_approved_users is true. Matched against the agent-session creator before firing linear-implement.

enforce_approved_users.description: When true, gate agent-session reply-approval on approved_user_ids (matched against the session creator). Default false — the GitHub merge is the real sign-off gate.

ceo.description: Config for the AI CEO (W-358) — the scheduled autonomous orchestration layer that runs the product day-to-day and reports to the human chairman. Optional: forks without this block simply never run the CEO. Consumed only by the meta-repo's .github/workflows/ai-ceo.yml (not by target repos).

enabled.description: Master switch. When false, the scheduled ai-ceo workflow fetches config, sees this is off, and no-ops (exit 0). Flip to true (then redeploy the Worker) to arm the CEO. This is the chairman's go-live gate.

briefing_marker.description: Prefix the CEO's briefing comment must start with. The ai-ceo verify step greps for it to confirm the run reported (house rule: no silent successes).

chairman_linear_handle.description: Linear @-handle of the human chairman. The CEO @-mentions this handle ONLY when a decision needs the human.

chairman_github_login.description: GitHub login of the human chairman (used when escalating PR-merge decisions).

digest_issue_id.description: Linear issue identifier (e.g. W-358) where the CEO posts its recurring briefing thread.

autonomy.description: What the CEO may do without asking the chairman. Chairman decision 2026-06-03: full autonomy is the design; auto_merge and approve_plans ship false so we can ramp safely (enable propose+ops first, watch, then grant code authority).

refine_and_prioritize.description: May edit descriptions, set priorities, relate/close issues.

delegate_to_loop.description: May kick off the implementation loop (move issues into the trigger state / delegate to @claude).

approve_plans.description: May approve agent plans on its own. Default false.

auto_merge.description: May merge PRs that pass ALL guardrails. Default false — the chairman's PR merge stays the gate until explicitly granted.

guardrails.description: Hard limits. A merge/approve action requires EVERY applicable guardrail to hold; otherwise the CEO escalates instead of acting.

max_delegations_per_run.description: Cap on issues delegated to the loop per CEO run.

max_actions_per_run.description: Cap on total mutating actions per CEO run (issue creates/edits/merges/delegations).

max_pr_additions.description: Auto-merge only PRs adding ≤ this many lines.

max_pr_files.description: Auto-merge only PRs touching ≤ this many files.

require_reviewer_approval.description: Auto-merge requires an APPROVE review from the reviewer bot.

require_all_checks_green.description: Auto-merge requires all GitHub checks green.

protected_paths.description: Glob/path prefixes the CEO must NEVER auto-merge a change to. Any PR touching one escalates to the chairman.

file.description: Repo-relative path of a JSON file to apply key-level protection to. The blanket `protected_paths` block does NOT need to list this file — listing it here is the opt-in to fragment-level evaluation.

paths.description: JSONPath-style keys inside `file` that are hard-protected. The CEO scans the PR's `+`/`-` lines for any matching literal JSON key (e.g. `"ceo":` for `$.ceo`); if none match, the file clears the guardrail, otherwise it escalates as a protected-path violation.

protected_json_paths.description: Fragment-level companion to `protected_paths`. For files listed here, the CEO performs key-level diff checks instead of blanket-blocking the whole file — so safe additive edits (e.g. adding a new top-level block to `config/pipeline.json`) can auto-merge while sensitive sections (the `ceo` block, auth/approval config, the guardrail config itself) stay hard-protected. Paths must resolve against the file's actual structure (e.g. `$.ceo.guardrails.protected_paths`, not `$.protected_paths`); a path that returns `null` on both versions will never trigger escalation and is a no-op.

escalation.description: When the CEO must stop and pull in the chairman rather than act.

always_escalate.description: Plain-language situations the CEO must always escalate.

sli.description: Config for the loop SLI eval harness (W-362) — the weekly scheduled job that aggregates Linear state history + GitHub PR data into a structured success-rate / mean-fix-cycles / stuck-rate digest the AI CEO survey reads. Optional: forks without this block ship the harness dormant.

tracking_issue_id.description: Linear issue identifier (e.g. W-376) where the harness posts its weekly metrics comment and where the CEO survey reads the latest one. When empty, eval-harness.yml no-ops (harness dormant).

window_days.description: Rolling window (in days) the harness aggregates over each run. Default 7 (weekly cadence).

project_to_repo.description: Map of Linear project UUID → GitHub `owner/name` repo. Worker's lookupRepo() routes repository_dispatch calls via this map.
